_comment: tiles2 — breaking change from tiles v1. Each ground type is a SPECIFICALLY named subfolder (saturated_grass, not 'grass') so more variants can be added later. Flow: generate -> raw/ (+ per-sheet metadata, kind=base|transition) -> postprocess copies into base/ or transitions/<other>/, normalizing brightness/hue/saturation to the ref-sprite(s). Raw is always kept so postprocess can be re-tuned and re-run.

prompt._comment: Quality-focused terrain prompts. We do NOT ask for lineless / no-outline — create-tiles-pro bakes an outline in regardless, and demanding it only degrades the art. tiles-pro reads a NUMBERED description as the tile-set variations; `style` sets the overall art direction.
prompt.style: cozy hand-crafted top-down RPG pixel art, rich natural texture, cohesive harmonious palette, soft directional shading, high quality game terrain tile
prompt.base_template: isometric {description} terrain tiles, every tile is {name}: {variations}. {style}
prompt.transition_template: isometric terrain tiles blending from {from_desc} to {to_desc}: {variations}. {style}
prompt.base_variations: 1) plain even ground 2) soft surface texture 3) a few natural details 4) a slightly darker weathered patch 5) a slightly lighter sunlit patch 6) small scattered specks
prompt.transition_variations: 1) mostly {from_name} with {to_name} entering from one corner 2) an even diagonal split from {from_name} to {to_name} 3) mostly {to_name} with a patch of {from_name} 4) a soft natural border between {from_name} and {to_name} 5) {to_name} scattered across {from_name} 6) a gradual blend from {from_name} into {to_name}

ground_types.name: dirt
ground_types.description: packed brown dirt with mixed light and dark patches

ground_types.name: grey mountain stone
ground_types.description: rugged grey mountain rock and stone

ground_types.name: wooden balcony
ground_types.description: warm wooden plank balcony floor / deck boards

_transitions_comment: Unordered NEEDED borders. The loop completes each type fully (bases, then a transition FROM this type TO every listed neighbour, stored under <type>/transitions/<neighbour>) before starting the next type. Each border is thus generated from both sides across the run; the neighbour side is normalised once that neighbour has a ref-sprite (re-run postprocess).

targets._comment: base_sheets_per_type raw BASE sheets accumulate first, THEN this type's transitions, before the next type. Each sheet = one create-tiles-pro item (~16 tiles). Sync (git<-PixelLab) drops sheets you delete in the UI, so the loop tops back up to target.

budget._comment: Proceed if the subscription pool is above min_generations_remaining OR there are >= min_usd credits (PixelLab bills USD credits once subscription generations hit 0; tiles are cheap).

postprocess._comment: raw -> base/transitions. neutralize_outline removes any residual dark rim (tiles2 = no outline). strength (0..1) scales colour-normalisation toward the ref-sprite(s).
